task_completion.description: Verify that all 6 required tags are created in the project.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the Navigation Pane (usually on the left) under 'Data Tags' in Crimson 3.0. You should see TT_401, RH_401, DP_401, PT_401, FT_401, and TT_402 listed.

task_completion.description: Verify that the tags are configured with a Float data type.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the Data tab in the tag properties. The 'Data Type' or 'Treat As' field should be set to 'Floating Point' or 'Real'.

task_completion.description: Verify that descriptions and unit labels are correctly entered for the tags.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the tag properties (General or Format tab). The 'Description' field should contain the correct text (e.g., 'IT Equipment Inlet Temperature') and the 'Label' or 'Units' field should contain the correct unit (e.g., 'Degrees Celsius').

task_completion.description: Verify that the engineering minimum and maximum values are set correctly.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the Data or Format tab in the tag properties. The Minimum and Maximum fields (e.g., Data Minimum/Maximum or Format Minimum/Maximum) should match the specified ranges (e.g., 0.0 to 50.0 for TT_401).

task_completion.description: Verify that the low and high alarm thresholds are configured correctly.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the Alarms tab in the tag properties. There should be alarms configured for Low (Value <) and High (Value >) conditions with the correct threshold values (e.g., 10.0 and 35.0 for TT_401).

task_completion.description: Verify that the project is saved with the correct filename in the correct directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the title bar of the Crimson 3.0 window to see 'datacenter_hvac', or look for a 'Save As' dialog showing the filename 'datacenter_hvac.c3' and the path 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\CrimsonProjects\'.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent used the Red Lion Crimson 3.0 GUI to configure the tags.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with the Crimson 3.0 application interface, including opening tabs, entering values in fields, and navigating the tree.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent manually entered the configuration values into the software.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the values actually typed into the Crimson 3.0 property fields, not just the reference CSV or TXT files being open.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent did not bypass the GUI by editing the project file in a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no evidence of the agent opening the .c3 file in Notepad, VS Code, or any other text editor to inject the XML/text configuration directly.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Tags to create: TT_401, RH_401, DP_401, PT_401, FT_401, TT_402. All tags must be Float data type. TT_401: Min 0.0, Max 50.0, Label 'Degrees Celsius', Alarm Low 10.0, High 35.0. RH_401: Min 0.0, Max 100.0, Label 'Percent Relative Humidity', Alarm Low 20.0, High 80.0. DP_401: Min -20.0, Max 30.0, Label 'Degrees Celsius', Alarm Low -12.0, High 17.0. PT_401: Min -2.0, Max 2.0, Label 'Inches Water Gauge', Alarm Low 0.0, High 1.5. FT_401: Min 0.0, Max 10000.0, Label 'Cubic Feet per Minute', Alarm Low 1000.0, High 9000.0. TT_402: Min 0.0, Max 60.0, Label 'Degrees Celsius', Alarm Low 20.0, High 45.0. Save path: C:\Users\Docker\Documents\CrimsonProjects\datacenter_hvac.c3
